jokes.text: Homer laughs at a sign and asks if anyone wants to know why it's funny
jokes.setup: Homer finds something hilarious that no one else understands
jokes.punchline: Family's unanimous 'No' response
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness paired with family's exasperation. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Homer always picks seven because 'there were seven apostles'
jokes.setup: Lisa reveals Homer always picks seven
jokes.punchline: Homer's incorrect biblical knowledge as justification
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident ignorance about religion is perfectly in character and gets a solid laugh.

jokes.text: 'Boy, that's a big staff. And still he wasn't that funny.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about apostles and their number
jokes.punchline: Homer treating Jesus like a comedian with a large entourage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion treating religious figures like show business. Meta humor about comedy itself.

jokes.text: Stupid prey. They never suspect anything... Ooh, a hotdog. [HOMER GRUNTING]
jokes.setup: Homer mocks prey for being stupid while reading about Venus flytraps
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately falls for the same trap, attracted by a hotdog
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Homer literally becomes the stupid prey he just mocked. Excellent structure and character integration.

jokes.text: 'Flower power my ass.'
jokes.setup: Moe's bar is empty due to the flower blooming event
jokes.punchline: Moe's bitter dismissal of the hippie phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Moe's cynical worldview and his resentment at losing customers.

jokes.text: 'Hey, those are my customers-slash-only friends.'
jokes.setup: Moe sees his bar customers at the botanical garden
jokes.punchline: Admitting they're his only friends reveals his loneliness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - the 'slash' construction makes it funnier while revealing Moe's pathetic social situation.

jokes.text: 'I've been replaced by a cooler?'
jokes.setup: Moe realizes his customers are drinking from a cooler instead of coming to his bar
jokes.punchline: His existential crisis about being replaced by an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Moe's insecurity and the absurdity of his professional jealousy.

jokes.text: Someone who's alone, already bitter... someone who's been trampled on so many times... one more won't make any... Oh, Moe.
jokes.setup: Security guard listing criteria for who to kick out
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's perfectly describing Moe who's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Well-structured buildup to the obvious but satisfying punchline. Good timing and delivery.

jokes.text: 'I'd prefer that you push me... seeing how I'm desperate for any human contact.'
jokes.setup: Given choice between walking out with dignity or being pushed
jokes.punchline: Moe chooses humiliation because it involves human touch
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Moe's desperate loneliness in a darkly funny way. The logic is both sad and hilarious.

jokes.text: 'Oh, man. The plant cut one.'
jokes.setup: Century flower releases terrible smell
jokes.punchline: Bart interprets it as the plant farting
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective toilet humor perfectly suited to Bart's character and age.

jokes.text: 'That smells worse than James Coco's ski boots.'
jokes.setup: Everyone reacting to the terrible smell
jokes.punchline: Moe's oddly specific celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Bizarre specificity makes it funny - who thinks of James Coco's ski boots? Very Simpsons random cultural reference.

jokes.text: Moe thinks he's found someone worse off than him (a baby in a carriage) but the mother appears
jokes.setup: Moe feeling sorry for himself finds what he thinks is an abandoned baby
jokes.punchline: Baby has loving parents, making Moe realize he's still the worst off
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup showing Moe's self-pity and how life keeps proving him right about his situation.

jokes.text: 'Well, at least we're outside instead of sitting at home watching TV.' 'I hear that. Hey, that car has a TV in it.'
jokes.setup: Homer philosophically appreciates being outdoors
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted by TV in car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - attempts at profundity immediately undermined by his base desires.

jokes.text: 'Well, now it's a didgeri-don't.'
jokes.setup: Character wants to play didgeridoo professionally
jokes.punchline: Granny's pun rejecting the idea
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works in the fake TV show context. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: 'A dream deferred is a dream denied.'
jokes.setup: Homer wants to watch baseball in someone's car
jokes.punchline: Homer uses profound-sounding phrase for trivial desire
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - applying lofty rhetoric to petty concerns. The mock-profundity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 'That hideous man is a hero.'
jokes.setup: Moe saves baby from car accident
jokes.punchline: Woman praises him while simultaneously insulting his appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly backhanded compliment that acknowledges Moe's heroism while being brutally honest about his looks.

jokes.text: 'They're standing up with no one in them.'
jokes.setup: Looking at newspaper photo of Moe
jokes.punchline: Lisa notices Moe's pants are so stiff they stand up empty
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy observation about Moe's questionable hygiene and clothing quality.

jokes.text: 'What's the gag? Is it full of chiggers?'
jokes.setup: Marge gives Moe a nice sweater as thanks
jokes.punchline: Moe can't believe someone would be genuinely nice to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe paranoia - his life has been so rough he can't accept genuine kindness. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Aw, jeez, there's something in my eye. Oh, it's just some glass.
jokes.setup: Moe seems to be getting emotional from Marge's kindness
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally has glass in his eye
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up emotional moment then deflates with absurd injury. Very economical.

jokes.text: 'The Swedish are coming! The Swedish are coming!'
jokes.setup: Grampa arrives in carpool
jokes.punchline: Grampa's bizarre Revolutionary War-style warning about Swedes
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa non-sequitur mixing historical references with modern paranoia. Perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: 'Look at me, I'm Speedy Alka Seltzer. Whoa!'
jokes.setup: Homer mentions Grampa's medication changes
jokes.punchline: Grampa acts out advertising mascot then presumably crashes
jokes.explanation: Typical Grampa physical comedy with pop culture reference. Serviceable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Look at me, I'm Speedy Alka Seltzer. Whoa!
jokes.setup: Grampa continuing his medication-induced rant
jokes.punchline: References old advertising mascot while presumably falling over
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference with physical comedy. Weak structure and cheap reference humor.

jokes.text: Moe dislocates his arm to entertain Maggie
jokes.setup: Moe trying to entertain baby
jokes.punchline: Uses painful self-injury as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Moe's desperate desire to connect. The self-harm for entertainment is perfectly Moe.

jokes.text: 'Raggedy Andy over there has been giving you the button eye.'
jokes.setup: Moe notices doll looking at Maggie
jokes.punchline: Treats doll like threatening male using 'button eye' pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with Moe's overprotective jealousy. The pun on 'evil eye' works well with the doll.

jokes.text: 'You know, it gave me kind of a good, warm feeling... like when you get drunk and fall asleep in snow.'
jokes.setup: Moe describing the good feeling of babysitting
jokes.punchline: Compares it to hypothermia from alcoholic blackout
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Moe's only reference point for good feelings is dangerously unhealthy. Perfect character writing.

jokes.text: 'I was gonna erase those apostrophes and replace them with G's.'
jokes.setup: Moe checking his busy schedule
jokes.punchline: His only plan is pointless graffiti correction
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing how empty Moe's life is. The specificity of the grammar vandalism is funny.

jokes.text: Why's she crying? Oh, that's right. I still got her nose. Here you go, you little idiot.
jokes.setup: Maggie is crying and Moe doesn't know why
jokes.punchline: Forgot he was playing 'got your nose' game, calls baby an idiot
jokes.explanation: Classic baby game with Moe's inappropriate language. The insult to a baby is characteristically harsh.

jokes.text: 'You calling her repellent?' / 'You ain't Karen Allen yourself, you know?'
jokes.setup: Woman says baby looks like Moe, he takes it as insult
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific celebrity comparison insult
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity reference typical of Simpsons. Karen Allen is an oddly specific choice for this insult.

jokes.text: 'Hey, wait. That's supposed to be me.' / 'You could be my father figure.' / 'No way. I'm not getting my fingerprints on that train wreck.'
jokes.setup: Homer realizes Moe is acting as Maggie's father figure
jokes.punchline: Homer refuses to be Bart's father figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - jealous about one kid but wants nothing to do with the problematic one.

jokes.text: 'I said pipe down, Amtrak.'
jokes.setup: Bart trying to help with advice
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses him with train-related insult
jokes.explanation: Weak callback to the train wreck line. The Amtrak connection feels forced and not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Maggie, look what's in your ear. A penny. Okay, we won't tell no one about that.
jokes.setup: Moe performing magic trick for baby
jokes.punchline: Implies the penny was actually in her ear, treats it as conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Dark implication that Moe actually found a penny in baby's ear. The conspiracy tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'No means no for Elmo.'
jokes.setup: Moe tickling Elmo toy
jokes.punchline: Children's toy uses consent language
jokes.explanation: Dark subversive humor putting adult concepts in children's toy. Very memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: 'This must be a takeoff on that Alice in Underpants movie I saw.'
jokes.setup: Moe looking at Alice in Wonderland book
jokes.punchline: Connects children's classic to pornographic film
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe's seedy worldview corrupting innocent content. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Ha. It's nice to be with someone... who can't understand the horrible things I say.
jokes.setup: Moe enjoying time with baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Admits he likes her because she can't judge his awful personality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment - Moe understanding exactly why he connects with a baby. Dark but insightful.

jokes.text: 'White rabbit, chicks popping mushrooms? This is like the Playboy Mansion.'
jokes.setup: Moe reading Alice in Wonderland
jokes.punchline: Interprets fantasy elements as party drugs and women
jokes.explanation: More of Moe's corrupted perspective. Consistent with character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: 'We could scare that movie producer... by putting a horse's blanket in his bed.' / 'Imagine waking up and seeing you got the wrong blanket.'
jokes.setup: Telling Godfather story to baby
jokes.punchline: Makes the horse head scene about wrong bedding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection making the famous Godfather scene mundane and domestic. Very clever subversion.

jokes.text: Moe puts orange in his mouth like Vito Corleone and growls at Maggie
jokes.setup: Moe telling Godfather story
jokes.punchline: Acts out the orange scene with baby as audience
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback to famous movie scene. Works as character moment showing Moe's inappropriate babysitting.

jokes.text: 'And he shuts the door on Annie Hall.'
jokes.setup: Ending Godfather story
jokes.punchline: Confuses Kay with Annie Hall from different movie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's confused movie knowledge. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Yeah, sure he was, Nahasapasa-I'm-Raising-A-Pervert.'
jokes.setup: Apu defends his son playing near Maggie
jokes.punchline: Moe insults Apu with mangled name pun
jokes.explanation: Clever play on Apu's hard-to-pronounce name. Shows Moe's overprotectiveness and casual cruelty.

jokes.text: [SINGING] The squirmy wormy spider Squirts out of Daddy's hands Daddy feels rejected He's gonna eat some cake
jokes.setup: Homer singing nursery rhyme to compete with Moe
jokes.punchline: Turns itsy-bitsy spider into pathetic story about parental rejection
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt to compete results in self-pitying nursery rhyme. Appropriately pathetic for the character.

jokes.text: 'Yeah, great present, Selma. Nice of you to break a five.'
jokes.setup: Selma gives baby a rattle
jokes.punchline: Moe sarcastically thanks her for cheap gift
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic comment about cheap gift. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: 'Ah, get a neck, Frankenstein.'
jokes.setup: Moe insults Selma's gift
jokes.punchline: Selma fires back about Moe's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good comeback about Moe's appearance. 'Get a neck' is a memorable insult construction.

jokes.text: 'I recorded that for private use.'
jokes.setup: Toy plays Homer saying 'I peed my pants'
jokes.punchline: Homer admits he recorded it for personal reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Homer's embarrassing admission makes the toy gift even more inappropriate.

jokes.text: 'Yeah, I had to. It's so weird watching the video and not getting any sound.'
jokes.setup: Moe has baby monitor in Maggie's room
jokes.punchline: Reveals he also has video surveillance
jokes.explanation: Escalates the creepy factor perfectly. Moe's casual admission makes it even more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: You have your own baby monitor in our child's room? Yeah, I had to. It's so weird watching the video and not getting any sound.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers Moe has baby monitor
jokes.punchline: Moe admits to also having video surveillance
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - bad situation gets worse as Moe reveals more surveillance. Good creepy comedy.

jokes.text: 'Last night at the bar, you called her Raquel.'
jokes.setup: Arguing about who cares more about Maggie
jokes.punchline: Moe points out Homer's mistake with baby's name
jokes.explanation: Good character point about Homer's neglect. The specific wrong name makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Is that, uh, "get out" like "leave"? Or "get out" as in, "Get out, you banged Bridget Fonda?"'
jokes.setup: Homer tells Moe to get out
jokes.punchline: Moe's delusional interpretation as excitement about celebrity encounter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Moe's desperate need for validation. The specific celebrity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: 'You guys mind if I, uh, kiss your tummies?'
jokes.setup: Moe feeling lonely without Maggie
jokes.punchline: Asks his bar customers for inappropriate baby-like affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing how Moe's paternal instincts have nowhere to go. Deeply uncomfortable but hilarious.

jokes.text: What are you screaming about? Did you discover my snake farm? Because I can explain. I'm going to farm and sell snakes.
jokes.setup: Marge screams about missing baby
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes it's about his secret snake farming venture
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with absurd Homer scheme. His matter-of-fact explanation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'I mean, freeze, scumbag. You can't write stuff like that.'
jokes.setup: Wiggum says 'Scum, freezebag' instead of 'Freeze, scumbag'
jokes.punchline: Comments on his own linguistic creativity
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment - his accidental wordplay followed by self-congratulation is very in character.

jokes.text: 'No. Now that I'm off duty, I'm the world's worst soccer coach. Ha.'
jokes.setup: Told he's the world's worst cop
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's only worst cop while on duty
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Wiggum's incompetence extends to all areas of his life.

jokes.text: Here's a clue. A discarded orange wedge. Orange wedge? [GROWLING]
jokes.setup: Moe finds clue to Maggie's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Orange wedge connects to Godfather story he told her
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that serves the plot. The orange wedge clue connects to Moe's storytelling.

jokes.text: 'I'm president of the Italian-American Anti-Defamation League. And this really burns my cannoli.'
jokes.setup: Confronting mobsters over stereotypical behavior
jokes.punchline: Uses Italian food metaphor for anger
jokes.explanation: Decent joke playing with Italian stereotypes while having Moe oppose them. The cannoli line is memorable.

jokes.text: 'Gee, ha. This is the second most guns ever pointed at me.'
jokes.setup: Multiple guns pointed at Moe during standoff
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions this isn't even his worst gun situation
jokes.explanation: Great character detail about Moe's dangerous life. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'I ain't cried like this since I paid to see Godfather III.'
jokes.setup: Tough mobster crying at Moe's speech about Maggie
jokes.punchline: Compares emotional reaction to terrible movie
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Godfather material. The idea of crying from disappointment rather than emotion is clever.

jokes.text: 'Oh, great. I'm caught in another tree.'
jokes.setup: Moe tries to leave dramatically
jokes.punchline: Gets stuck in tree again, ruining the moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that deflates the emotional moment. Shows Moe's consistent bad luck.
